title: Form Input Form Attributes
content:
### Input Form Attributes

These attributes on `<input>` override the `<form>` element's settings.

---

### Override Action, Method, Target

```html
<form action="/default" id="myform">
</form>

<!-- Input outside the form -->
<input type="submit" form="myform"
       formaction="/different-endpoint"
       formmethod="post"
       formtarget="_blank">
```

---

### Form Attributes for Input Fields

| Attribute | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `form` | Associates input with a form (by id) |
| `formaction` | Overrides form's `action` |
| `formmethod` | Overrides form's `method` |
| `formtarget` | Overrides form's `target` |
| `formenctype` | Overrides form's `enctype` |
| `formnovalidate` | Overrides form's validation |

---

### Input Outside Form

An input can be **outside** the `<form>` tag as long as it has a `form` attribute:

```html
<form id="signup" action="/register">
  <input type="text" name="username">
</form>

<!-- Outside, but still part of the form -->
<input type="email" name="email" form="signup">

<button type="submit" form="signup">Submit</button>
```

---

### Multiple Submit Buttons

```html
<form action="/submit">
  <input type="text" name="query">
  
  <button type="submit">Save</button>
  <button type="submit" formaction="/draft">
    Save as Draft
  </button>
</form>
```

---

> **Use Case:** The `form` attribute is useful when you need inputs outside the form for layout purposes. `formaction` is great for multiple submit buttons that send data to different endpoints.

quiz.question: Which attribute links an input to a form it is outside of?
